questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Step potential:
options: Voltage between two feet 1m apart during fault
questions.solution: Step potential: current through ground creates potential gradient. 1m step.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 100 W bulb and a 60 W bulb (rated 230 V) are connected in series across 230 V. Which glows brighter?
questions.solution: The 60 W bulb has higher resistance, so in series it drops more voltage and dissipates more power — it glows brighter.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 100 Ω, Q = 10 Ω and S = 30 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 100×30/10 = 300 Ω.

figure.caption: Wheatstone bridge

questions.solution: Due to line charging current. Compensated by shunt reactors.

questions.solution: Series reactor: limits short circuit current. Shunt reactor: absorbs reactive power.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The current-versus-frequency curve of a series RLC circuit peaks at the marked frequency f₀ ≈ 100 Hz. At this resonant frequency the power factor is:
questions.solution: At series resonance XL = XC, the impedance is purely resistive and current peaks, so the power factor is unity. (Here f₀ ≈ 100 Hz.)

questions.stem: For the series RLC circuit shown (L = 0.5 H, C = 10 µF), the resonant frequency is about:
